title: Selu
content:
{{Top|fa|leg|italics=1}} {{IndividualShip |image=[[File:Selu.jpg]] |name=''Selu'' |designer= |manufacturer=[[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|Corellian Engineering Corporation]]''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'' |line=[[YT-series/Legends|YT-series]] |model=Modified [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]] |type=[[Light freighter/Legends|Light freighter]] |cost= |modifier=*[[Azzameen family]][[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] **[[Aeron Azzameen]] |sysmods= |length=34.75 [[Meter/Legends|meters]]The ''[[Millennium Falcon Owner's Workshop Manual]]'' gives the length of a [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]] as 34.75 [[Meter/Legends|meters]]. |width= |height= |mass= |max accel= |mglt=88 [[Megalight|MGLT]] |max speed= |maneuverability=64 [[DPF/Legends|DPF]] |engine=Equipped |hyperdrive=Equipped |hdrange= |hdsystem= |poweroutput= |power= |shield gen=120 [[SBD/Legends|SBD]] |hull=76 [[RU/Legends|RU]] |sensor=Equipped |target=Equipped |navigation=Equipped |avionics= |maincomp= |countermeasures=*[[Chaff/Legends|Chaff]] {{C|optional}} *[[Flare/Legends|Flares]] {{C|optional}} |armament=*1 [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannon]] *1 [[Laser/Legends|laser]] [[Cannon/Legends|cannon]] [[Turret/Legends|turret]] *2 [[Warhead/Legends|warhead]] launchers |complement=*[[Tractor beam/Legends|Tractor beam]] {{C|optional}} *[[Sensor jammer/Legends|Sensor jammer]] {{C|optional}} |bays= |escapepods= |crew=Pilot {{C|1}} |skeleton= |passengers= |capacity= |cargohandling= |consumables= |lifesupport= |communications=Equipped |othersystems= |role= |commission= |destroyed=[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] ([[Great ReSynchronization|38:6:6–38:9]])The events of ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'' involving the ''Selu'' take place after the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] but before the [[Battle of the Bajic Shipyards]]. This [[Time/Legends|time]] period corresponds to the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. |retired= |battles= |affiliation=*Azzameen family *[[Twin Suns Transport Services/Legends|Twin Suns Transport Services]] |navy= |fleet= |owners=Azzameen family |captains=Aeron Azzameen |crewmembers= |registry= |aliases=''[[Venix]]'' {{C|during [[Raid on Viraxo 54|mission]] at [[Viraxo 54]]}} }} {{Quote|You talkin' cute with me, sister? I'll seize your ship right now!|Viraxo 54 security threatens Aeron Azzameen aboard the disguised ''Selu'' moments before the craft is disabled|audio=SeluTalkingCute-XWA.flac|}} The '''''Selu''''' was a [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]] owned by the [[Azzameen family]]. Along with its identical sister [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the ''[[Sabra]]'', it was utilized as a [[Transport/Legends|transport]] and [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] for family missions and assignments provided by [[Twin Suns Transport Services/Legends|Twin Suns Transport Services]], a [[Merchant/Legends|shipping]] company owned by the Azzameen family. In addition to the [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]], [[Deflector shield/Legends|shielding]], [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], and [[Turret/Legends|turret]] available to the [[YT-series/Legends|YT-1300 line]], the ''Selu'' was heavily modified to further give the ship protection from [[Pirate/Legends|raiding forces]] interfering with Azzameen affairs. [[Aeron Azzameen]], an adept [[Technician/Legends|technician]] and [[Slicer/Legends|slicer]], was often responsible for modifications to the [[Light freighter/Legends|light freighter]]; she also typically [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] the ship on family assignments. At the request of family patriarch [[Tomaas Azzameen/Legends|Tomaas Azzameen]], Aeron began training her youngest [[Gender/Legends|brother]], [[Ace Azzameen/Legends|Ace]], in the family trade shortly after the pivotal [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. Aeron flew the ''Selu'' while training Ace, aboard the ''Sabra'', by escorting Ace in delivery runs and inspection routines. In one such assignment, the Azzameen siblings discovered that [[Viraxo Industries]], a competitor to the Azzameens' shipping company, was actively attempting to [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotage]] the family. In retribution, Tomaas's brother and co-owner of Twin Suns Transport Services, [[Antan Azzameen]], developed a [[Raid on Viraxo 54|plan]] utilizing the ''Selu''. Renamed the ''[[Venix]]'' to avoid identification as an Azzameen craft, the masquerading ''Selu'' was disabled during an ill-fated raid on a [[Viraxo 54|Viraxo cargo facility]]. Ace was forced to destroy the stranded craft to keep the ship from falling into Viraxo control as he exited the [[Denbo system/Legends|system]] in the ''Sabra''. ==Description== {{Quote|You will fly the ''Sabra'', one of our family's modified Corellian YT-1300 transports. Aeron will fly an identical ship, the ''Selu''.|MK-09 briefs Ace Azzameen on his first mission|audio=SeluSabraIdentical-XWA.flac|}} The ''Selu'', owned, operated, and maintained by the [[Azzameen family]] and their company, [[Twin Suns Transport Services/Legends|Twin Suns Transport Services]], was a [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]] constructed by the [[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|Corellian Engineering Corporation]] in the [[YT-series/Legends|YT-series]] line of [[Light freighter/Legends|light freighters]]. The [[Starship/Legends|starship]], measuring 34.75 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in length, functioned as a [[Transport/Legends|transport]] as well as a [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]]; it could carry [[Cargo container/Legends|cargo containers]] ranging from [[cargo canister]]s to [[Class-L cargo container|Class-L types]] to and from [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] [[Space station/Legends|stations]]. The ''Selu'' could reach speeds in excess of 88 [[Megalight|MGLT]] and had a maneuverability rating of 64 [[DPF/Legends|DPF]]; a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] device was installed in the ship, allowing it to enter [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] to make its deliveries. The ''Selu''{{'s}} [[Hull/Legends|hull]] plating was rated at 40 [[RU/Legends|RU]], and it utilized a [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|deflector shield generator]] capable of providing 120 [[SBD/Legends|SBD]]. A forward-facing [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannon]], as well as a dorsal-mounted laser cannon [[Turret/Legends|turret]] able to be operated from the [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] or from the turret itself, provided the ''Selu'' with offensive capability against [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] or raiders. The ship could also mount additional systems for both offense and defense; [[warhead countermeasure]]s, beam devices, and [[Warhead/Legends|warhead]] launchers were optional units able to be added to the ship while in a [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]]. A [[Targeting computer/Legends|targeting system]], capable of tracking ships and projectiles, worked together with an onboard sensor system used to identify cargoes aboard ships and inside containers. [[Comlink/Legends|Communications equipment]] installed in the ship allowed the ''Selu''{{'s}} [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] to publicly address installations and hostile vessels. The systems could also be used for private communication with other Azzameen ships. Like its identical sister ship, the ''[[Sabra]]'', the ''Selu'' was modified by the Azzameens, including an additional seat in the cockpit of the craft. The freighter could also support the installation of fake [[Transponder code/Legends|transponder codes]], which allowed the Azzameens to change the ship's identity to infiltrate a rival company's supply line. A nameplate, containing its name and craft designation, was present aboard the ship. Externally, the ''Selu'' matched the ''Sabra'' in appearance; both craft also strongly resembled the well-known YT-1300 [[Tramp freighter/Legends|tramp freighter]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], sporting similar [[Color/Legends|red]] paint detail and plating arrangement. Family [[MK-series maintenance droid|droid]] [[MK-09/Legends|MK-09]] regarded both the ''Selu'' and ''Sabra'' as fine ships. ==History== ===Azzameen family service=== {{Quote|Incoming vessels are on an attack vector! System Patrol Craft ''Kouerd'' is abandoning us! They must have been bought off! Azzameen transports, we request your assistance.|Harlequin Station's director implores the visiting ''Selu'' and ''Sabra'' to assist against attacking raiders|audio=SeluHarlequinMayday-XWA.flac|}} The Azzameen family used the ''Selu'' around the period of the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]—a pivotal conflict in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] between the forces of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]— in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. During this time, it was mostly flown by [[Aeron Azzameen]] in missions for both the Azzameen family and their company, Twin Suns Transport Services. The family kept the ship parked in the hangar of their home, the orbital facility [[Azzameen Station/Legends|Azzameen Station]], located in a [[Azzameen Station system/Legends|system]] in the [[Garis sector/Legends|Garis sector]].''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' Following the Battle of [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]], family patriarch [[Tomaas Azzameen/Legends|Tomaas Azzameen]] decided it was time to teach his youngest [[Gender/Legends|son]], [[Ace Azzameen/Legends|Ace]], the family [[Merchant/Legends|trade]]. Aeron was asked to help train the young pilot. To do so, she flew in the ''Selu'' and relayed instructions to Ace, flying the ''Selu''{{'s}} sister ship, the ''Sabra'', alongside the family's modified [[Maintenance droid/Legends|maintenance droid]], MK-09. [[File:Ace1stmission-dat15210-1-XWA.png|thumb|left|220px|The ''Selu'' and ''Sabra'' approach Harlequin Station.]] For Ace's first mission behind the controls of a starship, Aeron taught her brother how to properly dock with and carry a cargo container. The ''Selu'' and ''Sabra'' were to make a trip through hyperspace to the trading [[XQ1 Platform|platform]] [[Harlequin Station]], deliver a pair of cargo canisters, and pick up and return a pair of [[Class-K cargo container]]s. Both ships deposited their cargo on the station and were moving to pick up the containers for the return trip when two [[Pursuer-class enforcement ship|''Pursuer''-class enforcement ships]], the ''[[Enkidu]]'' and the ''[[Gilgam]]'', flying for the Azzameens' rival company, [[Viraxo Industries]], arrived intending to raid Harlequin Station. After the station's hired security vessel, the [[IPV-1 System Patrol Craft|IPV-1]] ''[[Kouerd]]'', fled the [[Star system/Legends|system]], Harlequin Station's [[Director of Harlequin Station|director]] called on the ''Selu'' and ''Sabra'' for protection, as they were the only craft in the area. The two Azzameen freighters moved to intercept the interlopers and, after a brief [[Starfighter combat/Legends|dogfight]], forced the ''Pursuer''-class ships to leave, much to Harlequin Station's relief. The siblings collected their containers and returned to Azzameen Station without further incident. Later, after Aeron accidentally destroyed records for several areas in the [[Azzameen cargo fields|family's secret cargo fields]] during a security upgrade of her making, she again used the ''Selu'' and requested Ace's help in the ''Sabra'' to manually inspect the affected containers in order to rebuild the database. The mission began without incident, but upon arrival in the second area, the [[cargo ferry]] [[Raider (cargo ferry)|''Raider'']] was discovered attempting to heist a [[pressure tank]] filled with [[LOX]]. After trying unsuccessfully to hail the intruder, Aeron piloted the ''Selu'' to intercept the craft; with the ''Raider''{{'s}} exit blocked, the ''Sabra'' lined up a shot and obliterated the ''Raider''. With the pirate disposed of, the ''Selu'' and ''Sabra'' completed their scanning and moved to the last affected area. In this third area, the siblings discovered even more foreign presence in the formerly-secret cargo area: two [[Razor-class starfighter|''Razor''-class starfighters]] escorting the ''Enkidu''. Aeron and Ace both moved to once again remove the interloping ships. The ''Sabra'' challenged the ''Razor''-class [[Starfighter/Legends|fighters]] while the ''Selu'' charged the ''Enkidu'', currently docked with one of the family's containers. Although Ace and MK-09 were able to destroy the starfighters, the ''Enkidu'' escaped. Upon the ''Pursuer''-class ship's exit, the ''Selu'' and ''Sabra'' investigated the family's containers. Eventually, Ace discovered that the container the ''Enkidu'' had docked with was filled with illegal [[Spice/Legends|spice]]. To protect the family from Imperial investigation, Aeron piloted the ''Selu'' to retrieve the compromised container and return it to Azzameen Station, where the family would determine what should be done with it. ===Masquerading as the ''Venix''=== {{Quote|What about your ship? We cannot leave it behind for the Viraxo!''"
"''I guess that means you'll have to destroy it!|MK-09 and Aeron Azzameen discuss the fate of the stranded ''Selu''|audio=SeluLeaveBehind-XWA.flac|}} While the constant harassment of ships from Viraxo Industries—specifically, the ''Enkidu''—was already a problem for the Azzameens, the discovery that the Viraxo were actively attempting to sabotage Azzameen affairs pushed [[Antan Azzameen]], brother of Tomaas and co-owner of Twin Suns Transport Services, to develop a plan of retribution. Antan enlisted Aeron, known to the Azzameens as a skillful [[Slicer/Legends|slicer]], to install a fake transponder code into the ''Selu''. This code enabled the ''Selu'' to broadcast its identification as the ''[[Venix]]'', a frequent supplier to Viraxo Industries, in order to escape suspicion during the retaliatory strike. The ''Selu'', masquerading as the ''Venix'', was to deliver the container of illicit spice to the Viraxo [[Cargo Facility 1|cargo facility]] [[Viraxo 54]] in the [[Denbo system/Legends|Denbo system]] and depart while an Azzameen distraction elicited a visit by local Imperial authorities. Antan hoped the Empire would discover the spice-filled container in its routine after-raid inspection, creating strain between the Viraxo and their Imperial allies. [[File:AeronScarpers-XWA.png|thumb|right|200px|Aeron Azzameen abandons the doomed ''Selu''.]] Aeron piloted the disguised ship to Viraxo 54 with the illegal spice in a container that had been repainted to hide any connection to the Azzameen family or their company. With the falsified credentials holding, the ''Selu'' took place in the line of freighters waiting to deliver their cargo. Ace, piloting the ''Sabra'', and brother [[Emon Azzameen|Emon]], flying his [[Firespray-31-class patrol and attack craft/Legends|Firespray-31 craft]], ''[[Andrasta]]'', were to enter the system shortly after Aeron deposited the cargo and engage the station's ''Razor''-class fighters. Unfortunately for the Azzameen plan, the ''Selu''{{'s}} container deposit was delayed by a lengthy queue. The brothers arrived before the ''Selu'' had a chance to offload the container, and Aeron, noticing that her freighter was under increasing scrutiny, decided to flee the system before the ruse was discovered. She alerted Viraxo 54 of her intentions, citing the extenuating circumstances of the ongoing raid, but station security ordered the ''Selu'' to remain in the queue. Aeron, disagreeing with the station's assessment of the situation, disregarded the commands to stay, released the spice container, and piloted the ''Selu'' toward an exit vector. Before she could leave, however, Viraxo 54 disabled the ''Selu'' with a barrage of [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]] [[Ion blaster/Legends|blasts]]. Realizing her ship was now defenseless and stranded, Aeron called out to her brothers for rescue. Emon responded and Aeron abandoned the stranded YT-1300 freighter in a [[Environment suit/Legends|space suit]] before Emon picked her up in the ''Andrasta''. With Aeron aboard, Emon left the area and commanded the ''Sabra'' to destroy the ''Selu'' to keep the ship from falling under Viraxo control. Ace did so before exiting himself just as Imperial forces arrived. Though the plan did not proceed as intended and resulted in the loss of the ''Selu'', the Azzameen children and their uncle Antan considered the raid a victory. Following the mission, the ''Selu''{{'s}} original nameplate was gifted to Ace, who mounted it above a hallway entrance in his quarters. ==Commanders and crew== [[File:Aeron.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Aeron Azzameen]] {{Quote|We'll be using the YT-1300s. I'm taking the ''Selu''; you can fly the ''Sabra''.|Aeron Azzameen speaks to her brother Ace on an upcoming family assignment|audio=SeluTakingTheYTs-XWA.flac|}} The ''Selu'' was owned and operated by the Azzameen family. The YT-1300 was often piloted by Aeron Azzameen, who utilized the craft in multiple deliveries for the family. She also performed much of the modifications on both it and its sister ship, the ''Sabra'', including the installation of faked transponder codes. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|Fine ships.|MK-09, referring to the ''Selu'' and ''Sabra'' in his collection of Ace Azzameen's adventures|}} The ''Selu'' was featured in the [[1999]] [[Star Wars video games|PC game]] ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'' and its [[Prima Games]] [[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|strategy guide]]. In the game, the ship acted as a guide in the tutorial missions by showing the player how to pilot starships, inspect objects, and engage enemies. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Selu}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Selu |pl=Selu }} [[Category:Starships of Twin Suns Transport Services]] [[Category:YT-1300 light freighters]]
